questions.prompt: The first permanent English settlement in North America was established in 1607 at which location?
questions.correctText: Jamestown, Virginia

questions.prompt: The Mayflower Compact of 1620 is significant because it established the principle of
choices: self-government based on the consent of the governed
direct royal control of the colonies
questions.correctText: self-government based on the consent of the governed

questions.prompt: The Puritans who founded the Massachusetts Bay Colony in 1630 sought primarily to
choices: seek gold and silver in the New World
create a model religious community based on Calvinist principles
establish a haven for Catholic refugees
questions.correctText: create a model religious community based on Calvinist principles

questions.prompt: William Penn founded Pennsylvania in 1681 as a refuge primarily for members of which religious group?
questions.correctText: Quakers

questions.prompt: Which economic system, dominant in the colonial era, held that colonies existed to enrich the mother country?
questions.correctText: Mercantilism

questions.prompt: The Navigation Acts of the 17th century required colonial merchants to
choices: trade only with the Dutch Republic
ship most goods through English ports using English ships
purchase enslaved labor only from Spanish traders
questions.correctText: ship most goods through English ports using English ships

questions.prompt: The Southern colonies developed an economy based largely on
choices: plantation agriculture using enslaved African labor
fur trapping and the fur trade
questions.correctText: plantation agriculture using enslaved African labor

questions.prompt: The French and Indian War (1754–1763) ended with the Treaty of Paris in 1763, which
choices: granted Canada and lands east of the Mississippi to Britain
returned all French territories to France
established the United States as an independent nation
questions.correctText: granted Canada and lands east of the Mississippi to Britain

questions.prompt: The Proclamation of 1763 angered American colonists because it
choices: forbade colonial settlement west of the Appalachian Mountains
established the Anglican Church in all colonies
required colonists to quarter British soldiers
questions.correctText: forbade colonial settlement west of the Appalachian Mountains

questions.prompt: Britain ended its policy of salutary neglect after 1763 primarily to
choices: raise revenue to pay debts from the French and Indian War
questions.correctText: raise revenue to pay debts from the French and Indian War

questions.prompt: The Stamp Act of 1765 was significant in colonial history because it
choices: was the first direct tax imposed by Parliament on the colonies
abolished slavery in the northern colonies
questions.correctText: was the first direct tax imposed by Parliament on the colonies

questions.prompt: The colonial slogan 'No taxation without representation' was a direct response to
choices: Parliament's taxation of colonies without colonial members in Parliament
the Bank of the United States
questions.correctText: Parliament's taxation of colonies without colonial members in Parliament

questions.prompt: The Townshend Acts of 1767 imposed duties on which of the following?
choices: Imported goods such as glass, paper, paint, and tea
Land transactions in the western territories
Manufactured items produced in the colonies
questions.correctText: Imported goods such as glass, paper, paint, and tea

questions.prompt: The Boston Massacre of 1770 was used by colonial leaders such as Samuel Adams primarily to
choices: negotiate a peaceful settlement with Britain
create propaganda against British military presence
questions.correctText: create propaganda against British military presence

questions.prompt: The Boston Tea Party of December 1773 was a colonial protest against the
choices: Tea Act, which granted the East India Company a tea monopoly
questions.correctText: Tea Act, which granted the East India Company a tea monopoly

questions.prompt: The Intolerable Acts (Coercive Acts) of 1774 were intended by Parliament to
choices: punish Massachusetts and the port of Boston
establish religious freedom in the colonies
questions.correctText: punish Massachusetts and the port of Boston

questions.prompt: The First Continental Congress, which met in Philadelphia in 1774, was called primarily to
choices: coordinate a colonial response to the Intolerable Acts
questions.correctText: coordinate a colonial response to the Intolerable Acts

questions.prompt: The opening battles of the American Revolution took place in April 1775 at
questions.correctText: Lexington and Concord

questions.prompt: Thomas Jefferson's Declaration of Independence drew heavily on the political philosophy of
choices: John Locke and the doctrine of natural rights
questions.correctText: John Locke and the doctrine of natural rights

questions.prompt: According to the Declaration of Independence, the primary purpose of government is to
choices: secure the unalienable rights of the people
questions.correctText: secure the unalienable rights of the people

questions.prompt: The Battle of Saratoga in 1777 is considered a turning point of the American Revolution because it
choices: convinced France to enter the war as an American ally
led to the capture of George Washington
drove the British out of New York City
questions.correctText: convinced France to enter the war as an American ally

questions.prompt: Washington's army endured a harsh winter of 1777–1778 at which encampment in Pennsylvania?
questions.correctText: Valley Forge

questions.prompt: The French alliance of 1778 was significant to the American Revolution because it
choices: provided naval power, troops, and supplies to the American cause
questions.correctText: provided naval power, troops, and supplies to the American cause

questions.prompt: The decisive American victory of the Revolution occurred in 1781 at
questions.correctText: Yorktown, Virginia

questions.prompt: The Treaty of Paris of 1783 did which of the following?
choices: Returned the colonies to British control
Recognized American independence and set boundaries to the Mississippi River
questions.correctText: Recognized American independence and set boundaries to the Mississippi River

questions.prompt: A major weakness of the Articles of Confederation was that the national government could not
choices: levy taxes or regulate interstate commerce
questions.correctText: levy taxes or regulate interstate commerce

questions.prompt: Shays' Rebellion of 1786–1787 in Massachusetts is significant because it
choices: demonstrated the strength of the Articles of Confederation
convinced many leaders that a stronger national government was needed
led directly to the Declaration of Independence
questions.correctText: convinced many leaders that a stronger national government was needed

questions.prompt: The Constitutional Convention of 1787 originally met in Philadelphia to
questions.correctText: revise the Articles of Confederation

questions.prompt: The Great Compromise at the Constitutional Convention resolved the conflict between large and small states by
choices: creating a bicameral legislature with proportional representation in the House and equal representation in the Senate
questions.correctText: creating a bicameral legislature with proportional representation in the House and equal representation in the Senate

questions.prompt: The Three-Fifths Compromise at the Constitutional Convention dealt with the question of
choices: how enslaved people would be counted for representation and taxation
the location of the national capital
the establishment of the Supreme Court
questions.correctText: how enslaved people would be counted for representation and taxation

questions.prompt: Federalists supporting ratification of the Constitution argued primarily that
choices: a strong central government was necessary to preserve the union
states should retain all sovereign powers
the document should establish a monarchy
questions.correctText: a strong central government was necessary to preserve the union

questions.prompt: Anti-Federalists opposed ratification of the Constitution primarily because they
choices: feared the absence of a bill of rights and a too-powerful federal government
supported the Articles of Confederation as written
questions.correctText: feared the absence of a bill of rights and a too-powerful federal government

questions.prompt: The Bill of Rights, adopted in 1791, was added to the Constitution primarily to
choices: expand the powers of the federal government
protect individual liberties from federal infringement
questions.correctText: protect individual liberties from federal infringement

questions.prompt: Which amendment in the Bill of Rights guarantees freedom of speech, religion, press, assembly, and petition?
questions.correctText: The First Amendment

questions.prompt: George Washington's Farewell Address (1796) warned Americans to avoid
choices: permanent foreign alliances and political factions
questions.correctText: permanent foreign alliances and political factions

questions.prompt: Alexander Hamilton's financial program as Secretary of the Treasury included all of the following EXCEPT
choices: federal assumption of state Revolutionary War debts
a protective tariff to promote manufacturing
questions.correctText: immediate abolition of slavery

questions.prompt: The Whiskey Rebellion of 1794 was significant because it demonstrated that the federal government would
choices: use force to enforce federal law
questions.correctText: use force to enforce federal law

questions.prompt: The political division between Thomas Jefferson and Alexander Hamilton produced the first American
choices: two-party system, with Democratic-Republicans and Federalists
questions.correctText: two-party system, with Democratic-Republicans and Federalists

questions.prompt: The Supreme Court case Marbury v. Madison (1803) established the principle of
questions.correctText: judicial review

questions.prompt: The Louisiana Purchase of 1803 was significant because it
choices: doubled the size of the United States and secured the Mississippi River
added Florida to the United States
questions.correctText: doubled the size of the United States and secured the Mississippi River

questions.prompt: The Lewis and Clark expedition (1804–1806) was sent by President Jefferson primarily to
choices: explore the Louisiana Territory and seek a route to the Pacific
establish a permanent army post in Texas
questions.correctText: explore the Louisiana Territory and seek a route to the Pacific

questions.prompt: The War of 1812 between the United States and Britain was caused in part by
choices: British impressment of American sailors and interference with American trade
questions.correctText: British impressment of American sailors and interference with American trade

questions.prompt: Francis Scott Key wrote 'The Star-Spangled Banner' during the War of 1812 after witnessing the British bombardment of
questions.correctText: Fort McHenry in Baltimore

questions.prompt: The Monroe Doctrine of 1823 declared that
choices: the Western Hemisphere was closed to further European colonization
the United States would join European alliances
slavery would be banned in all territories
questions.correctText: the Western Hemisphere was closed to further European colonization

questions.prompt: Andrew Jackson's use of the 'spoils system' meant that he
choices: sold federal lands to settlers cheaply
rewarded political supporters with government jobs
doubled the size of the army
questions.correctText: rewarded political supporters with government jobs

questions.prompt: The Indian Removal Act of 1830 authorized the federal government to
choices: forcibly relocate Native American nations from the Southeast to lands west of the Mississippi
grant U.S. citizenship to all Native Americans
guarantee Native American land rights forever
questions.correctText: forcibly relocate Native American nations from the Southeast to lands west of the Mississippi

questions.prompt: The 'Trail of Tears' refers to
choices: the forced march of the Cherokee and other tribes from the Southeast to Oklahoma
the route of escaped enslaved people on the Underground Railroad
the path of the Lewis and Clark expedition
the migration of settlers to Oregon
questions.correctText: the forced march of the Cherokee and other tribes from the Southeast to Oklahoma

questions.prompt: The Nullification Crisis of 1832–1833 centered on South Carolina's claim that it could
choices: nullify federal tariff laws within its borders
questions.correctText: nullify federal tariff laws within its borders

questions.prompt: President Andrew Jackson's 'Bank War' resulted in the
choices: destruction of the Second Bank of the United States
founding of the New York Stock Exchange
passage of the Federal Reserve Act
questions.correctText: destruction of the Second Bank of the United States

questions.prompt: The Whig Party was formed in the 1830s primarily in opposition to
choices: the policies and authority of President Andrew Jackson
questions.correctText: the policies and authority of President Andrew Jackson

questions.prompt: The annexation of Texas by the United States in 1845 had been delayed primarily because of disputes over
choices: whether Texas would enter as a slave state
Texas's refusal to join the Union
questions.correctText: whether Texas would enter as a slave state

questions.prompt: The Oregon Trail was significant in the 1840s because it
choices: was the main path settlers used to reach the Pacific Northwest
linked the East to the Mississippi River
was a Civil War supply route
questions.correctText: was the main path settlers used to reach the Pacific Northwest

questions.prompt: The Mexican-American War (1846–1848) ended with the Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo, which
choices: restored all conquered lands to Mexico
transferred the Mexican Cession—including California and the Southwest—to the United States
established slavery in all new territories
guaranteed Mexican statehood in the United States
questions.correctText: transferred the Mexican Cession—including California and the Southwest—to the United States
questions.topic: Mexican-American War / Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo

questions.prompt: The discovery of gold at Sutter's Mill in 1848 sparked the California Gold Rush of 1849, which contributed to
choices: California's rapid population growth and statehood in 1850
the abolition of slavery in the South
the outbreak of the War of 1812
questions.correctText: California's rapid population growth and statehood in 1850

questions.prompt: The doctrine of Manifest Destiny in the 1840s held that the United States was destined to
choices: expand its territory across the North American continent
establish colonies in Africa and Asia
remain confined to the original thirteen states
questions.correctText: expand its territory across the North American continent

questions.prompt: William Lloyd Garrison was an influential abolitionist who
choices: edited the antislavery newspaper The Liberator
served as a Supreme Court justice
questions.correctText: edited the antislavery newspaper The Liberator

questions.prompt: Frederick Douglass was important to the abolitionist movement primarily because he
choices: led an armed slave revolt in Virginia
delivered powerful speeches and writings against slavery based on his own experience
questions.correctText: delivered powerful speeches and writings against slavery based on his own experience

questions.prompt: Harriet Tubman is best known for
choices: leading enslaved people to freedom on the Underground Railroad
writing the novel Uncle Tom's Cabin
leading the raid on Harpers Ferry
questions.correctText: leading enslaved people to freedom on the Underground Railroad

questions.prompt: The Underground Railroad was
choices: a secret network that helped enslaved people escape to free states and Canada
an early subway system in New York
questions.correctText: a secret network that helped enslaved people escape to free states and Canada

questions.prompt: The Seneca Falls Convention of 1848 is best remembered for producing the
choices: Declaration of Sentiments, which called for women's rights including suffrage
questions.correctText: Declaration of Sentiments, which called for women's rights including suffrage

questions.prompt: Elizabeth Cady Stanton and Lucretia Mott were leaders of which 19th-century reform movement?
questions.correctText: The women's rights movement

questions.prompt: Transcendentalist writers such as Ralph Waldo Emerson and Henry David Thoreau emphasized
choices: the importance of individualism, intuition, and a connection to nature
the necessity of slavery in society
the supremacy of the Catholic Church
questions.correctText: the importance of individualism, intuition, and a connection to nature

questions.prompt: The Missouri Compromise of 1820 attempted to settle the question of slavery in the territories by
choices: admitting Missouri as a slave state, Maine as a free state, and banning slavery north of 36°30'
abolishing slavery in the District of Columbia
creating popular sovereignty in all new territories
questions.correctText: admitting Missouri as a slave state, Maine as a free state, and banning slavery north of 36°30'

questions.prompt: The Compromise of 1850 included which controversial provision designed to appease Southern interests?
choices: A ban on slavery in Texas
questions.correctText: A stronger Fugitive Slave Act

questions.prompt: The Kansas-Nebraska Act of 1854 caused intense sectional conflict because it
choices: allowed popular sovereignty to decide slavery in territories north of the Missouri Compromise line
questions.correctText: allowed popular sovereignty to decide slavery in territories north of the Missouri Compromise line

questions.prompt: 'Bleeding Kansas' refers to the
choices: violent conflict between proslavery and antislavery settlers in Kansas Territory
Civil War battles in Kansas in 1864
Native American wars in the Plains
questions.correctText: violent conflict between proslavery and antislavery settlers in Kansas Territory

questions.prompt: Harriet Beecher Stowe's 1852 novel Uncle Tom's Cabin
choices: inflamed Northern opinion against the brutality of slavery
questions.correctText: inflamed Northern opinion against the brutality of slavery

questions.prompt: The Supreme Court's decision in Dred Scott v. Sandford (1857) ruled that
choices: African Americans were not citizens and Congress could not ban slavery in the territories
slavery was unconstitutional throughout the United States
questions.correctText: African Americans were not citizens and Congress could not ban slavery in the territories

questions.prompt: The Lincoln-Douglas debates of 1858 in Illinois centered chiefly on the issue of
choices: the expansion of slavery into the territories
questions.correctText: the expansion of slavery into the territories

questions.prompt: John Brown's raid on Harpers Ferry in 1859 was an attempt to
choices: seize a federal arsenal and incite an armed slave uprising
establish a free state in Kansas
questions.correctText: seize a federal arsenal and incite an armed slave uprising

questions.prompt: The election of Abraham Lincoln in November 1860 led directly to the
choices: secession of South Carolina and other Southern states from the Union
questions.correctText: secession of South Carolina and other Southern states from the Union

questions.prompt: The Confederate attack on Fort Sumter in April 1861 marked the
choices: beginning of the American Civil War
questions.correctText: beginning of the American Civil War

questions.prompt: The First Battle of Bull Run (Manassas) in July 1861 demonstrated to both sides that
choices: the Civil War would be longer and bloodier than expected
the Union army was vastly superior
questions.correctText: the Civil War would be longer and bloodier than expected

questions.prompt: The Battle of Antietam in September 1862 is significant because it
choices: was the bloodiest single day in American history and gave Lincoln the opportunity to issue the Emancipation Proclamation
led to the surrender of Robert E. Lee
questions.correctText: was the bloodiest single day in American history and gave Lincoln the opportunity to issue the Emancipation Proclamation

questions.prompt: The Emancipation Proclamation, effective January 1, 1863, declared free those enslaved
choices: in all of the United States immediately
in Confederate-held territory in rebellion against the Union
questions.correctText: in Confederate-held territory in rebellion against the Union

questions.prompt: Which two Union victories in July 1863 are generally considered the major turning points of the Civil War?
questions.correctText: Gettysburg and Vicksburg

questions.prompt: The Battle of Gettysburg (July 1863) was significant militarily because it
choices: ended General Robert E. Lee's last major invasion of the North
secured the Mississippi River for the Union
questions.correctText: ended General Robert E. Lee's last major invasion of the North

questions.prompt: The Union victory at Vicksburg in July 1863 was important because it
choices: gave the Union complete control of the Mississippi River
questions.correctText: gave the Union complete control of the Mississippi River

questions.prompt: In Lincoln's Gettysburg Address (November 1863), the president redefined the war's purpose as a struggle for
choices: a new birth of freedom and government of, by, and for the people
the abolition of the federal government
questions.correctText: a new birth of freedom and government of, by, and for the people

questions.prompt: General William Tecumseh Sherman's 'March to the Sea' in late 1864 was intended to
choices: destroy the South's will to fight by waging total war against civilian infrastructure
questions.correctText: destroy the South's will to fight by waging total war against civilian infrastructure

questions.prompt: Confederate General Robert E. Lee surrendered to Union General Ulysses S. Grant on April 9, 1865, at
questions.correctText: Appomattox Court House, Virginia

questions.prompt: President Abraham Lincoln was assassinated on April 14, 1865, by
choices: John Wilkes Booth at Ford's Theatre
Robert E. Lee in Washington, D.C.
Andrew Johnson at the White House
questions.correctText: John Wilkes Booth at Ford's Theatre

questions.prompt: The 13th Amendment to the Constitution, ratified in December 1865,
choices: abolished slavery throughout the United States
granted Black men the right to vote
questions.correctText: abolished slavery throughout the United States

questions.prompt: President Lincoln's plan for Reconstruction (the Ten Percent Plan) reflected his belief that
choices: the Southern states should be readmitted quickly and with leniency
the Confederate leadership should be executed
the Southern states should be governed permanently by the military
questions.correctText: the Southern states should be readmitted quickly and with leniency

questions.prompt: The Radical Republicans in Congress differed from Lincoln and later Andrew Johnson because they
choices: demanded stronger protection of freedmen's rights and a harsher Reconstruction of the South
supported the immediate readmission of Confederate states
questions.correctText: demanded stronger protection of freedmen's rights and a harsher Reconstruction of the South

questions.prompt: President Andrew Johnson was impeached by the House of Representatives in 1868 primarily because of his
choices: clashes with Radical Republicans over Reconstruction policy and his violation of the Tenure of Office Act
questions.correctText: clashes with Radical Republicans over Reconstruction policy and his violation of the Tenure of Office Act

questions.prompt: The 14th Amendment, ratified in 1868, did which of the following?
choices: Granted citizenship to all persons born or naturalized in the United States and guaranteed equal protection of the laws
Abolished slavery throughout the United States
Granted women the right to vote
questions.correctText: Granted citizenship to all persons born or naturalized in the United States and guaranteed equal protection of the laws

questions.prompt: The 15th Amendment, ratified in 1870, prohibited denying the right to vote on the basis of
choices: race, color, or previous condition of servitude
questions.correctText: race, color, or previous condition of servitude

questions.prompt: The Freedmen's Bureau, established by Congress in 1865, was created primarily to
choices: assist formerly enslaved people with food, schools, and labor contracts
questions.correctText: assist formerly enslaved people with food, schools, and labor contracts

questions.prompt: Sharecropping, which developed in the postwar South, was a system in which
choices: landowners provided land and supplies in exchange for a share of the crop
formerly enslaved people received forty acres and a mule
freedmen worked for fixed cash wages
questions.correctText: landowners provided land and supplies in exchange for a share of the crop

questions.prompt: Black Codes passed in Southern states after the Civil War were designed to
choices: restrict the rights and movements of freedmen and force them back into plantation labor
extend full citizenship to African Americans
establish public schools for all races
questions.correctText: restrict the rights and movements of freedmen and force them back into plantation labor

questions.prompt: The Ku Klux Klan, founded in 1866, used violence and terror primarily to
choices: intimidate African Americans and white Republicans and undermine Reconstruction
questions.correctText: intimidate African Americans and white Republicans and undermine Reconstruction

questions.prompt: The Compromise of 1877 ended Reconstruction by
choices: awarding the disputed presidency to Rutherford B. Hayes in exchange for withdrawing federal troops from the South
questions.correctText: awarding the disputed presidency to Rutherford B. Hayes in exchange for withdrawing federal troops from the South

questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the long-term significance of Reconstruction?
choices: It established lasting racial equality immediately
It produced constitutional amendments protecting civil rights that would be invoked in the 20th century, even as their promise was unfulfilled in the short term
It permanently barred Confederate states from rejoining the Union
questions.correctText: It produced constitutional amendments protecting civil rights that would be invoked in the 20th century, even as their promise was unfulfilled in the short term

questions.prompt: The Northwest Ordinance of 1787, one of the most important achievements of the Articles of Confederation, did which of the following?
choices: Established a process for territories to become states and prohibited slavery in the Northwest Territory
Abolished slavery in the entire country
questions.correctText: Established a process for territories to become states and prohibited slavery in the Northwest Territory

questions.prompt: The principle of federalism in the Constitution refers to
choices: the division of power between the national and state governments
the separation of church and state
the supremacy of the executive branch
questions.correctText: the division of power between the national and state governments

questions.prompt: The principle of separation of powers in the Constitution divides the federal government into
questions.correctText: legislative, executive, and judicial branches

questions.prompt: The system of checks and balances established in the Constitution is intended to
choices: prevent any one branch of government from becoming too powerful
ensure the supremacy of the judiciary
questions.correctText: prevent any one branch of government from becoming too powerful

questions.prompt: The Second Great Awakening in the early 19th century was a religious revival that contributed to
choices: the growth of reform movements such as abolitionism and temperance
the establishment of a national church
the founding of the Federalist Party
questions.correctText: the growth of reform movements such as abolitionism and temperance

questions.prompt: The Republican Party was founded in 1854 chiefly to
choices: oppose the expansion of slavery into the territories
defend the Bank of the United States
questions.correctText: oppose the expansion of slavery into the territories
